theoremName: augmented trace liminf representative compactness theorem
statement: Near-minimizing finite representatives for bounded augmented trace quotient coordinates have weak graph subsequential limits, and the augmented trace of the limit is the limiting trace coordinate.

weakRepresentativeCompactnessInputStatus.label: weak representative compactness input
weakRepresentativeCompactnessInputStatus.reason: The Mosco liminf theorem supplies weak graph compactness and graph-norm lower semicontinuity for bounded representative sequences.

traceLimitIdentificationInputStatus.label: trace limit identification input
traceLimitIdentificationInputStatus.reason: The augmented trace convergence theorem identifies the trace of the weak graph limit with the limiting augmented trace coordinate.

representativeCompactnessAndTraceIdentif.label: representative compactness and trace identification
representativeCompactnessAndTraceIdentif.reason: Near-minimizers are bounded in the graph norm, hence have weak graph limit points, and the closed augmented trace map identifies the limit trace.

proof: Choose finite representatives within epsilon_N of their finite quotient norms.
Bounded quotient norms give bounded representative graph norms.
Apply Mosco liminf weak compactness to extract a weak graph limit.
Apply closed augmented trace convergence to identify the limit coordinate.
Retain graph-norm lower semicontinuity for the abstract quotient-liminf principle.
